questions.question: Why are envelopes used in artificial synthesis of musical notes?

answers.a: To shape the amplitude dynamics of the note
answers.b: To alter the note's frequency content

explanations.a: Envelopes control the attack, decay, sustain, and release phases, thus shaping the amplitude contour over time to mimic natural instrument behavior.
explanations.b: Frequency content modification is managed separately and is not the primary function of envelopes.
explanations.c: Rhythm is governed by timing mechanisms, not by envelope settings.
explanations.d: Spatial characteristics are handled by different processing techniques.

answers.a: The process of smoothing out digital audio signals
answers.b: The effect where higher frequency components are misrepresented as lower frequencies due to insufficient sampling
answers.c: A technique to enhance the audio resolution
answers.d: The underlying algorithm for audio compression

explanations.a: Aliasing does not refer to a smoothing process; in fact, it creates unwanted distortions.
explanations.b: Aliasing occurs when a signal is sampled at a rate below its Nyquist frequency, causing high frequencies to fold back into lower frequencies.
explanations.c: Enhancing resolution is not aliasing but rather the opposite problem if sampling is inadequate.
explanations.d: Audio compression algorithms involve different principles and are not directly related to aliasing.

questions.question: In the simulation, what happens when you click the 'Listen' button?

answers.a: It visualizes the tone plot and plays the generated tone.
answers.c: It resets all configuration options to default.

explanations.a: Clicking 'Listen' triggers the simulation to both plot the envelope of the tone and play the tone according to the selected parameters.
explanations.b: Stopping the simulation is handled via different controls.
explanations.c: Resetting parameters is not linked to the 'Listen' function.
explanations.d: Experiment guidelines are available in the documentation, not through the 'Listen' button.

questions.question: In the simulation, synthesizing audio signals for which of the folowing musical intruments is not feasible?

explanations.c: Drums are percussion instruments whose spectrum is not harmonically structured.
